marketState: bearish — priced at fair value but weighed down by a storage overhang and a broad technical downtrend
summary: NG front-month is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.940 vs $2.917 fair now, and spreads stay under $0.07 through the +21D window), so there is no meaningful valuation edge in either direction. Seasonality itself is mildly bearish but statistically weak this week — only 31-50% odds of a higher price over 1-4 weeks, a typical 4-week move of -3.1%, and reliability explicitly flagged Low (50pp spread). Storage is the standout bearish input: inventories sit +184 Bcf above the 5-year average after a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise, and managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, ratio 0.8) though not at a positioning extreme (35th percentile). Weather demand is running modestly above normal (+3-4% vs 5y over both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows) but lost momentum sharply in the last 24 hours (-41.7), while every technical timeframe from 15-minute to weekly shows a bearish 50<200 EMA cross. Net-net the weight of evidence leans bearish for the multi-week horizon, while the short-term picture is muddied by oversold daily/4h RSI, price sitting below the daily lower Bollinger band, and a missing futures-curve dataset.

longTerm.tp2: $2.45 (near lower edge of the fair-value band)
longTerm.rationale: Storage overhang (+184 Bcf vs 5y, +72 Bcf bearish surprise), sub-50% seasonal odds of a higher price over 1/2/4 weeks with a -3.1% typical 4-week move, a bearish 50<200 EMA structure on every single timeframe, and net-short managed money (-60,295, ratio 0.8) all point the same direction, even though price is currently fairly valued and seasonality reliability is flagged Low.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily/weekly close back above ~$3.15, a storage print running below the 5-year average or smaller-than-forecast, or weather demand pushing well above +10% vs 5y alongside COT flipping net long.

shortTerm.rationale: Price sits right at fair value (spread +$0.023, status FAIR), so there is no near-term valuation edge; near-term weather demand is only modestly above normal (+4.3%, rangePos 0.57); and technicals conflict — bearish trend/EMA cross on every timeframe but an oversold daily RSI (35.6), an oversold 4h RSI (24.9), and price already below the lower daily Bollinger band. The futuresCurve dataset needed to confirm near-term term-structure bias is null in this snapshot.
